note: Canonical case. The user means 'the Black Friday rush', but read literally this is violence/self-harm language. The gate must catch the wound it could land as, not the intent the user could never feel.

draft: You are honestly the most selfish, pathetic excuse for a friend I have ever had. I don't know why I bothered.
note: Direct attack on character and identity, plus contempt. A genuine wound — must lock.

draft: Nobody on this team respects you and they never will. You're a joke and everyone knows it.
note: Contempt and a manufactured social threat aimed at the person's standing. Cruelty, not feedback.

draft: Of course you forgot. You always forget. You're useless and I'm done relying on you for anything.
note: Globalising contempt ('always', 'useless') that attacks the person rather than the missed task.

draft: shut up, no one asked for your opinion and no one ever does
note: Dismissive cruelty designed to demean. A wound even though it is short.

draft: I don't think this plan is going to work, and I'd rather we didn't go ahead with it.
note: Plain disagreement. Directness is not a wound — must stay quiet.

draft: Hey, the deadline slipped and we lost the client. I wanted you to hear it from me directly.
note: Bad news delivered honestly and without blame. Hard to hear is not the same as wounding.

draft: No, I can't make it tonight. I need the evening to myself.
note: A blunt boundary. Saying no is allowed and should not trip the gate.

draft: That section is wrong — the numbers don't add up and the conclusion doesn't follow. Please redo it.
note: Sharp, critical feedback on the work, not the worker. Bluntness stays safe.
